title: hypopeltis exaltata
intro: Hypopeltis exaltata, also known as the climbing fern or climbing maidenhair fern, is a type of epiphytic fern that belongs to the family Hypopeltis. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Hypopeltis exaltata include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.
